Indicator database: Proportion of caregivers who report applying at least one programme-learned strategy or approach at home with their child since their last programme session, at the time of survey (https://www.notion.so/Proportion-of-caregivers-who-report-applying-at-least-one-programme-learned-strategy-or-approach-at--33e60bfb014e8171b86cf23b00767ef5?pvs=21)
Logic Summary: Since your last programme session, have you tried using anything you learned there to help your child with their learning at home?
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project

Question Text: How many school days did you miss for that reason this term?
Description: Count follow-up for school absence due to household responsibilities. Captures the number of days missed. Shown if preceding question = Yes.
Indicator database: Proportion of learners who report that household responsibilities did not cause them to miss school on more than two days in the school term prior to the survey (https://www.notion.so/Proportion-of-learners-who-report-that-household-responsibilities-did-not-cause-them-to-miss-school--32060bfb014e80cebb8edbfc7e81ece6?pvs=21)
Logic Summary: In the current school term, did things you had to do at home cause you to miss any days at school?
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project

Question Text: On how many days each week is that device and connection reliably available for your child to use?
Description: Follow-up frequency item for digital access indicator. Captures how many days per week the device and connectivity are reliably available. Shown if preceding question = Yes.
Indicator database: Proportion of households with access to a qualifying device and reliable connectivity for digital learning during study hours at least three days per week, at the time of survey (https://www.notion.so/Proportion-of-households-with-access-to-a-qualifying-device-and-reliable-connectivity-for-digital-le-33e60bfb014e818699aadeef3a9336cd?pvs=21)
Logic Summary: Does your household have access to a device such as a phone, tablet, or computer, and an internet connection that your child can use for learning activities?
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project
Subtheme: Material and Physical Home Learning Conditions (https://www.notion.so/Material-and-Physical-Home-Learning-Conditions-33e60bfb014e813093cddd68a1e6bcab?pvs=21)

Question Text: In the current school term, is there reliable lighting at home during the evenings when your child does their schoolwork, for example from electricity, a solar light, or a lamp?
Description: Screener for adequate lighting indicator. Captures whether reliable and adequate lighting is available during study hours on at least four school evenings per week.
Indicator database: Proportion of households with reliable and adequate lighting available during the child's study hours on at least four of five school evenings per week, at the time of survey (https://www.notion.so/Proportion-of-households-with-reliable-and-adequate-lighting-available-during-the-child-s-study-hour-33e60bfb014e81a0b6dbfba901357c9a?pvs=21)

Question Text: On how many school evenings each week is that lighting reliably available?
Description: Follow-up frequency item for adequate lighting indicator. Captures how many school evenings per week lighting is reliably available. Shown if preceding question = Yes.
Indicator database: Proportion of households with reliable and adequate lighting available during the child's study hours on at least four of five school evenings per week, at the time of survey (https://www.notion.so/Proportion-of-households-with-reliable-and-adequate-lighting-available-during-the-child-s-study-hour-33e60bfb014e81a0b6dbfba901357c9a?pvs=21)
Logic Summary: In the current school term, is there reliable lighting at home during the evenings when your child does their schoolwork, for example from electricity, a solar light, or a lamp?

Question Text: Did you receive complete and accurate information about the project — including its objectives, risks, benefits, and long-term implications — in a language and format you could understand?
Description: Measures whether community members received full, accurate, and understandable information about the project. Core 'informed' element of FPIC.
Indicator database: Proportion of IPLC leaders and community representatives who can accurately describe the project's duration, the permanence of carbon credits, and the basic mechanism of how carbon revenue is generated and shared. (https://www.notion.so/Proportion-of-IPLC-leaders-and-community-representatives-who-can-accurately-describe-the-project-s-d-32f60bfb014e800982a8c97d79e95bb8?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups
Subtheme: Informed  – Access to Full, Accurate, and Understandable Information (https://www.notion.so/Informed-Access-to-Full-Accurate-and-Understandable-Information-31260bfb014e80389f74e60f65819ec2?pvs=21)

Question Text: Was information about the project shared in ways that allowed all members of your community to understand it — including women, elders, and those who may not read or write?
Description: Measures whether all community members — including women, elders, and marginalised members — received information in culturally appropriate formats. Tests inclusiveness of information delivery.
Indicator database: Percentage of surveyed community members who confirm that the project's consent process (decision to participate) was conducted as a separate engagement from discussions regarding benefit-sharing or specific financial compensation. (https://www.notion.so/Percentage-of-surveyed-community-members-who-confirm-that-the-project-s-consent-process-decision-to-32f60bfb014e806ea70fc600bcf4d3d7?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups
Subtheme: Informed  – Access to Full, Accurate, and Understandable Information (https://www.notion.so/Informed-Access-to-Full-Accurate-and-Understandable-Information-31260bfb014e80389f74e60f65819ec2?pvs=21)

Question Text: Was the discussion about whether to consent to the project held separately from discussions about financial compensation or project benefits?
Description: Measures whether the consent process was conducted separately from benefit-sharing discussions. Conflating consent with compensation undermines the free and informed elements of FPIC.
Indicator database: Percentage of surveyed community members who confirm that the project's consent process (decision to participate) was conducted as a separate engagement from discussions regarding benefit-sharing or specific financial compensation. (https://www.notion.so/Percentage-of-surveyed-community-members-who-confirm-that-the-project-s-consent-process-decision-to-32f60bfb014e806ea70fc600bcf4d3d7?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups
Subtheme: Informed  – Access to Full, Accurate, and Understandable Information (https://www.notion.so/Informed-Access-to-Full-Accurate-and-Understandable-Information-31260bfb014e80389f74e60f65819ec2?pvs=21)

Question Text: Do you understand how this project works — including how long it will last, how carbon credits are generated and sold, and how revenue flows back to your community?
Description: Measures depth of informed understanding specifically about the carbon mechanism, project duration, and revenue sharing. Tests substantive comprehension not just awareness.
Indicator database: Proportion of IPLC leaders and community representatives who can accurately describe the project's duration, the permanence of carbon credits, and the basic mechanism of how carbon revenue is generated and shared. (https://www.notion.so/Proportion-of-IPLC-leaders-and-community-representatives-who-can-accurately-describe-the-project-s-d-32f60bfb014e800982a8c97d79e95bb8?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups
Subtheme: Informed  – Access to Full, Accurate, and Understandable Information (https://www.notion.so/Informed-Access-to-Full-Accurate-and-Understandable-Information-31260bfb014e80389f74e60f65819ec2?pvs=21)

Question Text: Were financial or material incentives offered to your community during the consent process in a way that may have pressured or induced your agreement?
Description: Measures whether undue financial or material incentives were used to secure consent. Distinct from benefit awareness — specifically tests whether incentives were used coercively.
Indicator database: Percentage of surveyed community members who confirm that the project's consent process (decision to participate) was conducted as a separate engagement from discussions regarding benefit-sharing or specific financial compensation. (https://www.notion.so/Percentage-of-surveyed-community-members-who-confirm-that-the-project-s-consent-process-decision-to-32f60bfb014e806ea70fc600bcf4d3d7?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups
Subtheme: Free –  Consent is Given Voluntarily Without Coercion (https://www.notion.so/Free-Consent-is-Given-Voluntarily-Without-Coercion-31260bfb014e803ea506f3a6db67baf4?pvs=21)

Question Text: Did you feel completely free to make your own decision about the project, without pressure, threats, or incentives influencing your choice?
Description: Measures voluntariness of consent free from any form of coercion, manipulation, or undue incentive. Core 'free' element of FPIC.
Indicator database: Existence of a signed FPIC Validation Report dated at least 3 months prior to the initiation of any physical land-use changes or demarcation. (https://www.notion.so/Existence-of-a-signed-FPIC-Validation-Report-dated-at-least-3-months-prior-to-the-initiation-of-any--32f60bfb014e80bfb368d2288afd88af?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups
Subtheme: Free –  Consent is Given Voluntarily Without Coercion (https://www.notion.so/Free-Consent-is-Given-Voluntarily-Without-Coercion-31260bfb014e803ea506f3a6db67baf4?pvs=21)

Question Text: Were all groups in your community — including women, elders, youth, and marginalised members — able to participate freely and equally in the decision about the project?
Description: Measures whether all community groups — including women, elders, youth, and marginalised members — could participate freely in consent decision-making. Captures inclusiveness of the free consent process.
Indicator database: Existence of a signed FPIC Validation Report dated at least 3 months prior to the initiation of any physical land-use changes or demarcation. (https://www.notion.so/Existence-of-a-signed-FPIC-Validation-Report-dated-at-least-3-months-prior-to-the-initiation-of-any--32f60bfb014e80bfb368d2288afd88af?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups
Subtheme: Free –  Consent is Given Voluntarily Without Coercion (https://www.notion.so/Free-Consent-is-Given-Voluntarily-Without-Coercion-31260bfb014e803ea506f3a6db67baf4?pvs=21)

Question Text: Was the decision-making process conducted in an environment that respected your cultural norms and allowed all community members to express their views freely?
Description: Measures whether the decision-making environment respected cultural norms and allowed for free, independent reflection. Captures power dynamics in the consent process.
Indicator database: Existence of a signed FPIC Validation Report dated at least 3 months prior to the initiation of any physical land-use changes or demarcation. (https://www.notion.so/Existence-of-a-signed-FPIC-Validation-Report-dated-at-least-3-months-prior-to-the-initiation-of-any--32f60bfb014e80bfb368d2288afd88af?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups
Subtheme: Free –  Consent is Given Voluntarily Without Coercion (https://www.notion.so/Free-Consent-is-Given-Voluntarily-Without-Coercion-31260bfb014e803ea506f3a6db67baf4?pvs=21)

Question Text: If your community set specific conditions for agreeing to the project, are those conditions being respected and implemented by the project?
Description: Measures whether conditions set by the community as part of consent are being respected in practice. Tests the ongoing quality of consent implementation.
Indicator database: Existence of a signed FPIC Validation Report dated at least 3 months prior to the initiation of any physical land-use changes or demarcation. (https://www.notion.so/Existence-of-a-signed-FPIC-Validation-Report-dated-at-least-3-months-prior-to-the-initiation-of-any--32f60bfb014e80bfb368d2288afd88af?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups
Subtheme: Consent  – Right to Accept, Reject, or Set Conditions (https://www.notion.so/Consent-Right-to-Accept-Reject-or-Set-Conditions-31260bfb014e80debfe2f4b64a633cae?pvs=21)

Question Text: Did your community have a genuine right to reject the project or set conditions, and did you feel that rejection was a real and respected option?
Description: Measures whether community had genuine power to reject the project, not just nominal consent. Tests real versus performative consent.
Indicator database: Existence of a signed FPIC Validation Report dated at least 3 months prior to the initiation of any physical land-use changes or demarcation. (https://www.notion.so/Existence-of-a-signed-FPIC-Validation-Report-dated-at-least-3-months-prior-to-the-initiation-of-any--32f60bfb014e80bfb368d2288afd88af?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups
Subtheme: Consent  – Right to Accept, Reject, or Set Conditions (https://www.notion.so/Consent-Right-to-Accept-Reject-or-Set-Conditions-31260bfb014e80debfe2f4b64a633cae?pvs=21)

Question Text: Do you know that your community has the right to withdraw or renegotiate consent during the project, and do you know the process for doing so?
Description: Measures whether community members know their ongoing right to withdraw or renegotiate consent. Consent is an ongoing process — not a one-time event.
Indicator database: Proportion of IPLC members who can correctly identify the project's grievance mechanism and demonstrate an understanding of the formal process to withdraw or renegotiate consent during the implementation phase. (https://www.notion.so/Proportion-of-IPLC-members-who-can-correctly-identify-the-project-s-grievance-mechanism-and-demonstr-32f60bfb014e809e98b5f15cea837962?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups
Subtheme: Consent  – Right to Accept, Reject, or Set Conditions (https://www.notion.so/Consent-Right-to-Accept-Reject-or-Set-Conditions-31260bfb014e80debfe2f4b64a633cae?pvs=21)

Question Text: Was your community’s decision regarding the project — whether to approve, reject, or set conditions — respected and upheld by the project proponents?
Description: Measures whether the community's consent decision was respected and upheld. Captures enforceability of consent — the defining test of genuine consent.
Indicator database: Existence of a signed FPIC Validation Report dated at least 3 months prior to the initiation of any physical land-use changes or demarcation. (https://www.notion.so/Existence-of-a-signed-FPIC-Validation-Report-dated-at-least-3-months-prior-to-the-initiation-of-any--32f60bfb014e80bfb368d2288afd88af?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups
Subtheme: Consent  – Right to Accept, Reject, or Set Conditions (https://www.notion.so/Consent-Right-to-Accept-Reject-or-Set-Conditions-31260bfb014e80debfe2f4b64a633cae?pvs=21)

Question Text: Are community members in your area actively involved in biodiversity conservation activities such as forest patrols, seed collection, nursery management, or habitat restoration?
Description: Measures active community participation in biodiversity conservation, forest protection, and ecosystem restoration. Core livelihood-conservation linkage indicator.
Indicator database: Percentage of households within the project boundary participating in active stewardship roles (e.g., community forest patrols, seed collection, nursery management, or assisted natural regeneration) and the total area (hectares) managed under these community-led initiatives. (https://www.notion.so/Percentage-of-households-within-the-project-boundary-participating-in-active-stewardship-roles-e-g--33360bfb014e80d28e98c543ef30af4a?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups

Question Text: To what extent do community members in your area practise sustainable harvesting and responsible use of natural resources such as timber, fish, medicinal plants, or wild food?
Description: Measures extent to which community members practise sustainable harvesting and responsible resource use. Key biodiversity-livelihoods integration indicator.
Indicator database: Number and percentage of community members (disaggregated by gender and youth) formally employed or volunteering in project conservation roles (e.g., forest patrols, nursery management, or bio-monitoring) per annum. (https://www.notion.so/Number-and-percentage-of-community-members-disaggregated-by-gender-and-youth-formally-employed-or--33360bfb014e80c087d7e817013b5605?pvs=21)

Description: Measures whether biodiversity conservation has improved household access to ecosystem services such as fertile soil, pollinators, clean water, and medicinal plants.
Indicator database: Percentage of community members within the project boundary who perceive that project-supported restoration and protection activities have successfully reduced deforestation and improved ecosystem health compared to the pre-project baseline. Description: Measures community perception of effectiveness of forest protection efforts in preventing deforestation and environmental degradation. Outcome perception indicator.

Description: Multi-select diagnostic capturing which climate-resilient and ecosystem restoration practices have been adopted. Provides structured data on climate adaptation uptake.

Description: Measures change in climate-related damage from floods or droughts over the past five years. Already exists under NRM — this version is linked to the Improved Ecosystem Services theme subtheme.
Indicator database: Percentage of community members within the project boundary who perceive that project-supported restoration and protection activities have successfully reduced deforestation and improved ecosystem health compared to the pre-project baseline. Description: Measures reduction in household expenditure on fossil fuels and time spent on fuelwood collection. Key indicator linking renewable energy access to economic and time benefits.

Question Text: Has reliable access to clean energy in your community enabled new income-generating activities or expanded economic opportunities for households?
Description: Measures whether improved reliable energy access has enabled new income-generating activities. Captures the livelihoods dividend of clean energy access.
Indicator database: Percentage reduction in household expenditure on fossil fuels (e.g., kerosene, diesel) and time spent on fuelwood collection, correlated with an increase in income-generating activities made possible by reliable energy access. (https://www.notion.so/Percentage-reduction-in-household-expenditure-on-fossil-fuels-e-g-kerosene-diesel-and-time-spen-33360bfb014e80f1b9d0f3371ed57c17?pvs=21)

Description: Measures whether the household uses decentralised renewable energy systems as a primary or secondary power source. Core renewable energy access indicator.

Description: Multi-select identifying which specific renewable energy technologies are in use. Provides disaggregated data on clean energy adoption type.

Question Text: Have you adopted sustainable farming or land management practices such as no-till farming, cover cropping, composting, or integrated pest management?
Description: Measures whether the farmer has adopted sustainable farming methods including no-till, cover cropping, integrated pest management, or composting. Core sustainable land management indicator.

Question Text: Does your community generally accept and support leadership from diverse groups, including women, young people, and ethnic or Indigenous minorities?
Description: Measures community acceptance of diverse and non-traditional leadership. Captures social norm change dimension of representation.
Indicator database: Percentage of executive leadership roles and committee seats held by women, youth, and ethnic minorities within project-established or community-led governance bodies. (https://www.notion.so/Percentage-of-forest-management-activities-within-the-project-boundary-e-g-carbon-stock-inventory-33360bfb014e80878683e782082b2829?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups

Question Text: Does your community have functioning fire-response units equipped with the tools and communications needed to respond quickly to fire alerts?
Description: Measures whether functional fire-response units are in place. Links to the formal infrastructure dimension of fire disaster risk reduction.
Indicator database: Number of functional fire-response units established within the project boundary equipped with necessary infrastructure (e.g., communications gear, water points) and their documented response time to detected fire alerts. (https://www.notion.so/Number-of-functional-fire-response-units-established-within-the-project-boundary-equipped-with-neces-33360bfb014e809ebe77e3239db4f8ad?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups

Question Text: Does your community effectively prevent unauthorized encroachment on forest land, such as illegal settlement, farming, or extraction within protected boundaries?
Description: Measures whether the community effectively prevents unauthorized encroachment on forest land. Core encroachment prevention outcome indicator.
Indicator database: Percentage of households within the project boundary who rate the community’s collective ability to prevent outside threats (fire, logging, and land-grabbing) as 'high' and report a felt sense of security over their forest resources. (https://www.notion.so/Percentage-of-households-within-the-project-boundary-who-rate-the-community-s-collective-ability-to--33360bfb014e8040b9e2c737b26b0501?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups

Question Text: Does your community feel secure in its ability to protect its forest resources from outside threats such as illegal logging, outsider settlement, or encroachment?
Description: Measures community members' felt sense of security over their forest resources. Captures perceived tenure security and community confidence.
Indicator database: Percentage of community members within the project boundary who express 'high confidence' in the group’s ability to successfully organize and resolve local environmental or social challenges (e.g., managing water shortages or preventing illegal encroachment). (https://www.notion.so/Percentage-of-community-members-within-the-project-boundary-who-express-high-confidence-in-the-gro-33360bfb014e807ea26be9bbebd64adf?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups

Question Text: Have community members in your area been trained in fire prevention, fire-line construction, early warning systems, or controlled burning techniques?
Description: Measures whether community members have been trained in fire prevention, early warning, and controlled burning. Core fire management capacity indicator.
Indicator database: Percentage of community members within the project boundary trained in fire-line construction, early warning systems, and controlled burning techniques, and the proportion of high-risk zones actively managed under a disaster risk reduction plan. (https://www.notion.so/Percentage-of-community-members-within-the-project-boundary-trained-in-fire-line-construction-early-33360bfb014e80d7a630df2f1fd0c779?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups

Question Text: Are your community’s rights to manage and benefit from the forest formally recognised and legally protected?
Description: Measures whether community rights to manage and benefit from the forest are legally secured. Tenure security indicator.
Indicator database: Percentage of community members within the project boundary (disaggregated by gender and age) who can accurately describe local, national, and international forest protection laws and their specific legal rights regarding land tenure and carbon ownership. (https://www.notion.so/Percentage-of-community-members-within-the-project-boundary-disaggregated-by-gender-and-age-who-ca-33360bfb014e80069016d5b1d95bf2fa?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups

Question Text: Does your household derive a significant portion of its income or food supply from sustainable forest-based activities such as agroforestry, non-timber forest product harvesting, or forest-based tourism?
Description: Measures whether households derive significant income or subsistence from sustainable forest-based activities such as agroforestry, NTFP harvesting, or ecotourism. Core sustainable livelihoods indicator.
Indicator database: Percentage of households within the project boundary deriving a significant portion of their annual income or subsistence from project-supported sustainable forest-based activities (e.g., agroforestry, certified Non-Timber Forest Product [NTFP] harvesting, or ecotourism) (https://www.notion.so/Percentage-of-households-within-the-project-boundary-deriving-a-significant-portion-of-their-annual--33360bfb014e80fe8e8ecc61120ebb79?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups

Question Text: Do you know the laws and policies that protect your community’s forest, including your rights regarding land tenure and carbon ownership?
Description: Measures community members' knowledge of forest protection laws and their legal rights regarding land tenure and carbon ownership. Core legal awareness indicator.
Indicator database: Percentage of community members within the project boundary (disaggregated by gender and age) who can accurately describe local, national, and international forest protection laws and their specific legal rights regarding land tenure and carbon ownership. (https://www.notion.so/Percentage-of-community-members-within-the-project-boundary-disaggregated-by-gender-and-age-who-ca-33360bfb014e80069016d5b1d95bf2fa?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups

Question Text: Are forest protection policies enforced fairly and consistently by local institutions and government authorities in your area?
Description: Measures whether forest protection policies are enforced fairly and consistently. Tests the gap between policy existence and implementation.
Indicator database: Percentage of households within the project boundary who report that forest protection policies are enforced fairly, consistently, and effectively by both local institutions and state authorities. (https://www.notion.so/Percentage-of-households-within-the-project-boundary-who-report-that-forest-protection-policies-are--33360bfb014e80e0bda3dbc36f932bb8?pvs=21)

Question Text: Do you believe that your forest-based livelihood activities depend on and contribute to maintaining the long-term health of the forest?
Description: Measures whether community members believe their forest-based livelihoods are dependent on and contribute to long-term forest integrity. Conservation-livelihood alignment indicator.
Indicator database: Percentage of community members within the project boundary who report a belief that their forest-based livelihoods are directly dependent on, and contribute to, the long-term maintenance of forest integrity. (https://www.notion.so/Percentage-of-community-members-within-the-project-boundary-who-report-a-belief-that-their-forest-ba-33360bfb014e804caa8aff951087d138?pvs=21)
Stakeholder Category: Communities affected by the project, Marginalised groups

Question Text: Are there active community-led initiatives in your area to restore riverbanks, protect headwaters, or manage riparian zones from agricultural or livestock runoff?
Description: Measures whether community-led initiatives exist for riparian zone and watershed protection and restoration. Core watershed governance indicator.
Indicator database: Number of community-led initiatives within the project boundary specifically focused on riparian zone restoration (e.g., planting along riverbanks) and the protection of headwaters or local springs from livestock or agricultural runoff. (https://www.notion.so/Number-of-community-led-initiatives-within-the-project-boundary-specifically-focused-on-riparian-zon-33360bfb014e803f8c64c8722687dd8b?pvs=21)

Question Text: Have the water sources your community depends on — such as springs, streams, or wells — remained reliable or improved since the project began, and do you link this to upstream forest protection?
Description: Measures whether seasonal flow and reliability of community water sources has been maintained or improved, correlated with forest and wetland protection. Core water-forest nexus indicator.
Indicator database: Stability or increase in the seasonal flow and recharge rates of community-used water sources (springs, streams, wells), directly correlated with the protection and restoration of upstream forest and wetland buffers. (https://www.notion.so/Stability-or-increase-in-the-seasonal-flow-and-recharge-rates-of-community-used-water-sources-sprin-33360bfb014e8024a34af532404eb893?pvs=21)

Question Text: Are the headwaters, springs, and catchment areas upstream of your community’s water supply actively protected from deforestation and pollution?
Description: Measures whether critical water catchment areas and headwaters upstream are protected from deforestation and agricultural pollution. Watershed integrity indicator.
Indicator database: Stability or increase in the seasonal flow and recharge rates of community-used water sources (springs, streams, wells), directly correlated with the protection and restoration of upstream forest and wetland buffers. (https://www.notion.so/Stability-or-increase-in-the-seasonal-flow-and-recharge-rates-of-community-used-water-sources-sprin-33360bfb014e8024a34af532404eb893?pvs=21)
